config_stamp.hardware_gpu: Intel Arc 140V (16 GB, integrated, not used for this benchmark)
config_stamp.cryptography_backend: PyCA cryptography (AES-256-GCM via AESGCM, Intel AES-NI path)
config_stamp.cipher: AES-256-GCM, 96-bit random nonce per operation, 128-bit auth tag
config_stamp.dek_sealing: SoftwareSealer (same CPU path as TpmSealer-unsealed cipher; sealer does not affect per-field crypto latency)
config_stamp.methodology: time.perf_counter() wrapping individual cipher calls; 1000 iterations per cell; no co-resident GPU load; SQLite on local NTFS (temp dir)

single_field_overhead_us.description: Per-call AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt latency by content size

store_operation_overhead_ms.description: End-to-end SQLite read + decrypt latency for store API calls

list_sessions_50_sessions.note: Decrypts 50 AES-GCM title blobs per call

get_session_turns_10_turns.note: Decrypts 10 AES-GCM content blobs per call

not_measured: Disk I/O (SQLite page cache fully warm for all measurements)
DEK unsealing latency at process start (single-call cost, not per-operation)
plaintext get_session_turns baseline (measured separately as needed)

interpretation.absolute_overhead: 0.10 ms additional for list_sessions over 50 sessions
interpretation.ratio_vs_llm_inference: GPU inference latency ~1000-8000 ms per turn; encryption overhead is 3-5 orders of magnitude smaller
interpretation.aes_ni_note: Intel AES-NI hardware acceleration is used transparently by cryptography package on Core Ultra 7 258V
